author: @Beardshire
text:
"This shields pretty good!"

Bec de Corbin - "We'll see about that."

replies.author: @sevenproxies4255
replies.text:
To be fair, the Bec seems to rip through almost anything.

It's a beast of a close combat weapon

replies.author: @Aufsammelkabbler
replies.text:
Tod from Tods Workshop did some testing with wooden shields made as historically accurate as possible and they caught the arrows while still having quite a deep penetration. 
Catching the arrow with your shield is a detriment since it is quite hard to dislodge them and they make using the shield hard if not unusable in actual combat.
I think I would prefer one of those temu shields in an actual medieval battlefield.
The wooden ones wouldn‘t stop an axe spike ether and against the Bec all hope is lost no matter what you choose.
They can catch blades though which can be an advantage in close combat though

replies.author: @sevenproxies4255
replies.text: ​@AufsammelkabblerI think you'd rather catch an arrow in your shield rather than catching one in your torso.

replies.author: @codyslaughter4678
replies.text: ​​@sevenproxies4255 it's almost like it's a specialized tool designed to get through hard or resilient shells to the soft gooey treat on the inside.  Like a "crows break", as it were.... 😂

author: @darkshine5
text: Always interested in more ways to defend myself from armed robberies from working in a bottleshop. More everyday wearable anti-stab armour reviews would be very much appreciated. Edit I think testing with a compound bow against these armours would be valuable information

replies.author: @LillatOOsa
replies.text: This. And maybe test with modern crossbows also (Cobra RX Adder for example).

replies.author: @darkshine5
replies.text: ​@LillatOOsacrossbows require a firearms license in all Australian state except the Northern Territory. Compound bows are not licensed in Australia therefore are an actual threat.... Not that I am saying any part of archery should be restricted rather the opposite. I am anti Australian firearms/weapons laws as a licensed firearms owner we are far far far too strict.... We can't have pepper spray FFS

replies.author: @LillatOOsa
replies.text: @darkshine5 Thanks for the info. I thought Finland had strict weapon laws. Here crossbows of all kinds including repeating ones as well as swords are completely unregulated. What comes to firearms you can acquire rifles (bolt lock and semiautomatic up to 10 bullet magazine capacity) and shotguns if you have a hunting license which is easy to get, and age requirement is 15. Buying an assault rifle requires you to participate in "applied reservist shootings" for 12 times within two years with borrowed gun, and then you can apply for permit to acquire semiautomatic assault rifle.

author: @Chraan
text: You might encounter knives and machetes in self defense encounters, but it's not like you wouldn't see any impact weapons as well in this situation. To my knowledge wrenches and especially crowbars are also often encountered there. If you test for the sake of self defense, it would make sense to include these two tools as well!

author: @phantomkitsunezero
text:
I'd say your 'Philosophy or Use' for the average situation is accurate.
The round shield and a S T I C K puts you in a good place with pretty simple capability without training needed. You could reasonably fend off someone with a knife, even more so if you pickup some basic attacks like a shield bash or thrust as even a dull edge would hurt.

replies.author: @ThirtytwoJ
replies.text: not so much. aluminum doesnt have much weight or hardness to do more than irritate someone. its light but too awkward to wanna carry and realistically impractical.. wouldnt get it on while bein attacked. good for practice but little else.

replies.author: @robo5013
replies.text: Someone with a knife isn't going to thrust at your shield they are going to grab it and use it to control your arm while stabbing around it. Shields are pretty useless against in close weapons like daggers. This is coming from experience not speculation.
